verdict: The occupation is at a moderate risk of displacement by AI.

reasoning_steps.title: Routine Cognitive Tasks
reasoning_steps.body: Tasks 1, 2, 6, and 10 are routine cognitive tasks that can be automated by AI.

reasoning_steps.title: Creative Judgment
reasoning_steps.body: While creative judgment is required in some aspects of software development, it is not as prominent as other skills.

reasoning_steps.title: Regulatory Moat
reasoning_steps.body: There is no strong regulatory moat protecting the occupation from AI displacement.

adjacent_careers.name: Data Scientist
adjacent_careers.reasoning: Both roles involve working with data, but data scientists focus more on analysis and interpretation.

adjacent_careers.name: Quality Assurance Engineer
adjacent_careers.reasoning: While some QA tasks can be automated, human judgment is still required to identify defects.

recent_developments.headline: New AI-powered code completion tool released
recent_developments.impact: The tool uses machine learning to predict and complete code, potentially reducing the workload of human developers.

recent_developments.headline: AI coding assistants see broad enterprise adoption
recent_developments.impact: Major firms report 25–30% of new code is now AI-generated and reviewed by engineers.

quotes.quote: Software engineering will be very different by the end of 2025. Most code will be written by AI, with human engineers reviewing and directing.
quotes.speaker: Dario Amodei
quotes.title: CEO, Anthropic

quotes.quote: AI is going to be the biggest technological shift of our lifetimes. Bigger than mobile, bigger than the web. Inside Microsoft, more than 30 percent of the code in some projects is now written by AI.
quotes.speaker: Satya Nadella
quotes.title: CEO, Microsoft

quotes.quote: More than a quarter of new code at Google is generated by AI, then reviewed and accepted by engineers.
quotes.speaker: Sundar Pichai
quotes.title: CEO, Google / Alphabet
